exercises.name: Plank Hold
exercises.description: Hold plank position on forearms, maintain straight body line. 3 sets of 30-60 seconds.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Keep body completely flat - mimics streamlined position. Engage core to prevent hips dropping. Head neutral, looking down.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Exact position for backstroke - body flat, hips high. Keep head neutral (mimics head position in water). Core engaged to prevent hips sagging.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Core engaged for glide phase. Flat body position during stroke. Prevents hips from dropping during pull-kick sequence.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: THE essential butterfly drill. Mimics the whole-body undulation. Chest press initiates motion (same as in water). Feel the wave travel through body. Hips rise when chest drops.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: Lie on side, prop up on forearm, hold position. 30 seconds each side.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Develops core stability for rotation. Feel the side-body engagement that powers rotation. Keep hips high - don't let them sag.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Core stability for rotation. Side-body engagement for 30-45 degree body roll. Keep hips aligned.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Core stability for stroke rhythm. Side engagement supports pull-kick timing.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Side core engagement for undulation stability.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.description: Lie on back, arms up, legs up at 90 degrees. Lower opposite arm/leg alternately.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Core control for stable body position. Keep lower back pressed to floor - mimics keeping hips high in water. Controlled movement.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Core control on back - same position as backstroke. Back pressed to floor mimics flat body position.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Simulates the pull-kick coordination. Arms pull down (pull phase), legs extend and snap (kick phase). Feel the timing.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Core undulation from different angle. Feel the hip movement that powers dolphin kick. Core controls the wave motion.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.description: On hands and knees, extend opposite arm forward and opposite leg back. Hold 5 seconds each side.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Core stability with rotation. Opposite arm/leg mimics the diagonal connection in freestyle - arm reach opposite to kick. Keep core engaged, no rotation.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Core stability and balance. Opposite arm/leg coordination mimics backstroke rhythm. Keep back flat.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Core control for pull-kick timing. Opposite arm/leg develops the coordination needed for breaststroke rhythm.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Core stability for undulation base. Hands/knees position mimics the stable core needed during butterfly. Keep back flat.

exercises.name: Reverse Plank
exercises.description: Lie on back, prop up on hands and heels, body straight. Hold 30-45 seconds.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Back and core strength for body position. Opposite of regular plank - develops posterior chain. Keep body flat, hips high.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Primary core drill for backstroke. Body flat on back surface, hips elevated. Exact backstroke body position. Hold with core, not just arms.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Back strength for glide phase. Posterior chain development.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Back and core for undulation. Posterior chain supports wave motion.

exercises.name: Superman Hold
exercises.description: Lie face-down, arms extended forward, lift arms and legs. Hold 15-30 seconds. 3 sets.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Back strength for body position. Keep body long and engaged. Mimics the extended position during stroke.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Back strength for body position on back. Keep body long and engaged. Mimics the extended position during stroke.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Streamline position strength. Long body position during glide. Core engaged to maintain alignment.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Combines extension with undulation. Feel the transition from extended position to wave motion. Core connects upper and lower body.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Rotational Throws
exercises.description: Stand with feet planted, rotate torso to throw med ball against wall or to partner.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Rotate from HIPS and CORE, not just shoulders. Feel the power transfer from hips through torso to arms - same as freestyle rotation power.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Rotation power for backstroke. Rotate from hips/core, not just arms. Feel the hip-led rotation power.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Core rotation power. Hip-led movement for pull phase.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Full body power transfer. Core connects arm pull to dolphin kick.

exercises.name: Russian Twist
exercises.description: Sit on floor, lean back slightly, rotate torso touching floor on each side with hands or med ball.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Core rotation strength. Keep hips stable while rotating above - same as keeping legs steady while body rotates. Continuous motion.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Core rotation for body roll. Keep hips stable while rotating torso. Same as keeping legs steady while body rolls.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Core rotation for pull phase power.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Core undulation control.

exercises.name: Standing Rotation Drill
exercises.description: Stand tall, rotate shoulders and hips together 35-50 degrees each side, no arm swing. 20 reps.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Feel the hip-led rotation. Shoulders and hips move as a unit - exactly what you need in freestyle. Practice within your natural rotation range.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Practice the rotation angle (30-45 degrees). Feel the unit rotation - shoulders and hips together.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Unit rotation for pull phase.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Full body coordination.

exercises.name: High Elbow Catch Drill
exercises.description: Stand at wall, simulate catch motion: arm forward, drop fingertips, lift elbow. 20 reps each arm.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Practice the high elbow position muscle memory. Feel the fingertips dropping while elbow stays high - this is EVF. Do it slowly to feel the position.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Pinky-first entry position. High elbow during catch phase.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: High elbow during outsweep. Elbows stay above hands.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: High elbow catch for keyhole pull pattern.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Pull
exercises.description: Attach band to fixed point, stand facing it. Pull band back with high elbow, accelerate through. 3 sets of 15.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Simulates pull phase mechanics. High elbow throughout, accelerate at end. Feel the back muscles (lats) doing the work, not just arms.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Backstroke pull mechanics. Feel a relatively straight pull path (not exaggerated S-curve). Accelerate through the motion. Finish at hip.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Build pull strength through the pattern. Feel the acceleration during insweep. Keep elbows above hands. Quick recovery forward.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Build pull strength. Feel the acceleration through pull. High elbows throughout. Power from back muscles (lats), not just arms.

exercises.name: Shoulder Blade Squeeze
exercises.description: Stand with arms at sides, squeeze shoulder blades together, hold 5 seconds. 3 sets of 10.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Develops scapular stability for proper arm mechanics. Keeps shoulders stable during stroke. Prevents rounded shoulders.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Shoulder health for backstroke. Scapular stability prevents impingement.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Stable shoulders during pull and recovery.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Scapular stability for arm mechanics. Stable shoulders during pull and recovery. Prevents shoulder injury from butterfly stress.

exercises.name: Arm Circle Stretch
exercises.description: Large arm circles forward and backward, controlled. 10 each direction.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Shoulder mobility for recovery and entry. Keep motion smooth and controlled. Prevents impingement, develops range of motion.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Shoulder mobility for recovery. Prevents impingement.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Shoulder mobility for arm recovery forward.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Shoulder mobility for recovery. Relaxed arms during recovery. Prevents tension that causes fatigue.

exercises.name: Arm Pull Simulation
exercises.description: Stand, simulate stroke-specific arm motion. 20 reps.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Practice catch and pull muscle memory. High elbow throughout, accelerate through.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Practice full arm cycle muscle memory. Feel: pinky enters first, relatively straight pull path, thumb leads recovery.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Practice the pull motion muscle memory. Feel: hands turn out, narrow outsweep, elbows high during insweep, quick shoot forward. Do slowly to feel each phase.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Practice full arm cycle muscle memory. Feel: fingertips enter, high elbow catch, keyhole pull pattern, low relaxed recovery. Do slowly to feel each phase.

exercises.name: Pinky Entry Drill
exercises.description: Stand, simulate backstroke arm recovery: thumb leads up, rotate to pinky-down for entry. 20 reps each arm.

strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Practice the thumb-to-pinky rotation muscle memory. Pinky enters first every time - this is critical for backstroke. Feel the hand rotation.

exercises.name: Bent-Arm Recovery Drill
exercises.description: Stand, simulate backstroke arm recovery with bent elbow leading, hand trailing below. Keep arm close to body. 20 reps each arm.

strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Bent-arm recovery during recovery - elbow leads, hand trails below. Arm close to body line - not wide. Feel the relaxed, efficient motion.

exercises.name: Shoulder External Rotation
exercises.description: Hold resistance band, elbows at sides bent 90 degrees, rotate hands outward. 3 sets of 15.

strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Shoulder health for backstroke. External rotation strength protects shoulders during pinky-first entry. Prevents impingement.

exercises.name: Low Recovery Drill
exercises.description: Stand, simulate butterfly recovery with arms low and relaxed, slightly bent. 20 reps.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Practice low recovery muscle memory. Arms stay LOW over water level (imaginary). Relaxed, not rigid. Bent slightly, not straight. Feel the efficiency.

exercises.name: Lat Stretch
exercises.description: Arm up against wall, lean away. Hold 30 seconds each side.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Lat flexibility for arm reach and pull. Lats are primary pulling muscles. Flexible lats allow full arm extension.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Lat flexibility for reach and pull.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Lat flexibility for arm extension.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Lat flexibility for full arm range. Lats are primary butterfly muscles. Flexible lats allow full reach and powerful pull.

exercises.name: Ankle Flexibility Stretch
exercises.description: Sit on floor, legs extended. Point toes hard, then flex feet back. Hold each 10 seconds. 3 sets.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Develops flexible ankles for propulsion. Pointing = plantar flexion (foot pointed during kick). Flexing = dorsiflexion (builds range).
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Ankle flexibility for kick propulsion. Pointed toes during kick = propulsion. Flexible ankles = efficient kick.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Ankle range for bringing heels to buttocks. Flexible ankles allow proper kick recovery. Tight ankles limit knee bend.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Ankle speed for dolphin kick propulsion. Quick ankle motion essential. Dolphin kick needs fast, flexible ankles.

exercises.name: Calf Raises
exercises.description: Stand on edge of step, rise up on toes, lower below step level. 3 sets of 15.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Ankle strength and flexibility. Strong calves power the kick. Range of motion develops ankle flexibility.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Calf and ankle strength for kick. Strong calves power flutter kick. Builds ankle stability.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Ankle strength for whip kick power.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Ankle strength for dolphin kick.

exercises.name: Ankle Circles
exercises.description: Sit or lie down, rotate ankles in circles. 20 circles each direction each foot.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Ankle mobility for efficient kick. Loose, flexible ankles create propulsion. Tight ankles create drag.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Ankle mobility and flexibility. Loose ankles are essential. Each letter = full range of motion.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: General ankle mobility. Whip kick requires complex ankle motion. Full range of motion essential.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Ankle mobility for efficient kick.

exercises.name: Ankle Point/Flex Rapid
exercises.description: Sit, point toes hard, then flex back rapidly. 3 sets of 30 rapid switches.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Ankle speed for dolphin kick propulsion. Quick ankle motion essential. Dolphin kick needs fast, flexible ankles.

exercises.name: Ankle Alphabet
exercises.description: Sit, trace alphabet with toes. Full range of motion.

strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Ankle mobility and flexibility. Loose ankles are essential. Each letter = full range of motion.

exercises.name: Ankle Eversion Stretch
exercises.description: Sit on floor, legs extended. Turn feet outward forcefully. Hold 10 seconds. 5 sets.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: THE most critical drill for breaststroke. Feet MUST turn outward during kick. Develops the motion that makes legal whip kick. Feel the outside edge of foot engaging.

exercises.name: Sit on Heels Outward
exercises.description: Kneel, sit back on heels with feet turned outward. Hold 20-30 seconds. 3 sets.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Ankle flexibility in everted position. This is exactly the position feet need during whip kick propulsive phase. Builds flexibility over time.

exercises.name: Ankle Dorsiflexion Stretch
exercises.description: Stand facing wall, foot back, press heel down while flexing ankle. Hold 30 seconds each foot.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Ankle range for bringing heels to buttocks. Flexible ankles allow proper kick recovery. Tight ankles limit knee bend.

exercises.name: Whip Kick Simulation
exercises.description: Stand on one leg, simulate whip kick motion with other leg: heel to bum, foot out, snap together. 10 reps each leg.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Practice the kick motion muscle memory. Feel: heels to bum (not knees), narrow knees, feet outward, snap together. Slow motion to feel each phase.

exercises.name: Dolphin Plank
exercises.description: Start in plank, press chest down toward floor, hips rise up, return. Continuous rhythm. 3 sets of 15.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: THE essential butterfly drill. Mimics the whole-body undulation. Chest press initiates motion (same as in water). Feel the wave travel through body. Hips rise when chest drops.

exercises.name: Cat-Cow
exercises.description: On hands and knees, alternate between arching back up (cat) and dipping down (cow). 3 sets of 15.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Spine flexibility for undulation. Develops the wave motion through spine. Feel the chest press and hip lift sequence.

exercises.name: Standing Dolphin Kick
exercises.description: Stand tall, simulate dolphin kick motion from hips. Small amplitude, hip-driven. 20 reps.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Practice hip-driven kick motion. NOT from knees - feel the hips initiating. Keep knees relatively straight. Power from glutes and core.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge Pulse
exercises.description: Lie on back, knees bent. Bridge up, then pulse hips up and down rapidly. 3 sets of 20 pulses.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Hip drive strength for dolphin kick. Glutes power the kick. Feel the rapid hip motion. Builds endurance for continuous kicks.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat. Lift hips by squeezing glutes. Hold 3 seconds. 3 sets of 15.

strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Hip lift strength - keeps hips high in backstroke. Strong glutes prevent sitting position in water. Feel the hip elevation.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge with Adduction
exercises.description: Lie on back, knees bent. Bridge up, squeeze knees together at top. 3 sets of 15.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Combines hip extension with knee squeeze. Mimics the snap phase of whip kick. Feel the final squeeze power.

exercises.name: Adductor Stretch
exercises.description: Sit, feet together, knees out. Lean forward gently. Hold 30 seconds.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Hip flexibility for narrow knees during kick. Flexible adductors allow knees to stay close together. Tight adductors force knees wide = drag.

exercises.name: Hip Internal Rotation
exercises.description: Sit with knees bent at 90 degrees, feet wider than knees. Let knees fall inward. Hold 30 seconds.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Hip mobility for whip kick mechanics. Internal rotation needed for bringing heels to buttocks. Develops range of motion.

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description: Kneeling lunge position, push hips forward. Hold 30 seconds each side.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Hip flexibility for proper rotation range. Tight hip flexors limit rotation. Freestyle needs approximately 35-50 degrees of rotation each side.
strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Hip flexibility for rotation. Backstroke needs approximately 30-45 degree rotation each side. Flexible hips allow proper rotation.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Hip flexibility for bringing heels to buttocks. Flexible hips allow proper knee bend. Tight hips force knees wide.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Hip flexibility for dolphin kick range. Dolphin motion needs flexible hips. Tight hips limit undulation.

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Deep Stretch
exercises.description: Deep kneeling lunge, push hips forward. Hold 30 seconds each side.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Hip flexibility for dolphin kick range. Dolphin motion needs flexible hips. Tight hips limit undulation.

exercises.name: Ankle Mobility All Directions
exercises.description: Sit, move ankles through all ranges: point, flex, turn in, turn out, circles. 2 minutes each foot.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Comprehensive ankle mobility. Dolphin kick needs ankles that move freely in all directions. Build complete flexibility.

exercises.name: Knee-to-Chest Stretch
exercises.description: Stand, pull knee to chest. Hold 30 seconds each leg.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Hip flexibility for bringing heels to buttocks. Flexible hips allow proper knee bend. Tight hips force knees wide.

exercises.name: Lower Back Stretch
exercises.description: Lie on back, knees to chest, rock gently. 30 seconds.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Back flexibility for wave-style motion. Flexible spine allows undulation. Prevents back strain during breaststroke.

exercises.name: Chest Stretch
exercises.description: Stand in doorway, arms on door frame, lean forward. Hold 30 seconds.

strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Chest flexibility for arm reach. Open chest allows full arm extension. Tight chest limits entry position.

exercises.name: Shoulder Stretch
exercises.description: Pull arm across chest with other hand. Hold 30 seconds each arm.

strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Shoulder flexibility for recovery. Open shoulders allow proper arm position. Prevents shoulder strain.

exercises.name: Plank to Dolphin
exercises.description: Start in plank, press chest toward floor, hips rise, return. 10 reps.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Mimics wave-style breaststroke body motion. Chest press down = hips rise. Feel the undulating motion used by elite swimmers.

exercises.name: Streamline Stretch
exercises.description: Stand tall, arms extended overhead together. Hold 20-30 seconds.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: Practice streamline position muscle memory. Arms tight together, body tall. Feel the position you'll use off every wall.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Streamline position for glide phase. Arms tight together, body tall. Feel the glide position.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Streamline position for entry and glide. Arms tight, body tall. The position for every entry.

exercises.name: Wall Head Position Drill
exercises.description: Lie on floor, head on small cushion. Look straight up at ceiling. Hold 1 minute.

strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Practice neutral head position. Head resting naturally - not forced up or down. This is the position for backstroke.

exercises.name: Chin Tuck Drill
exercises.description: Stand or sit, gently tuck chin slightly. Hold 5 seconds, release. 10 reps.

strokeFocusPoints.backstroke: Neutral neck position. Chin slightly tucked, not forced. Prevents looking forward during backstroke.

exercises.name: Breaststroke Sequence Drill
exercises.description: Stand, practice: Pull (arms) → Breathe (lift chin) → Kick (simulate) → Glide (pause). 20 reps.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Practice the timing sequence muscle memory. Say it aloud: 'Pull, Breathe, Kick, Glide.' Feel the pause during glide. No rushing.

exercises.name: Jump Squat with Sequence
exercises.description: Squat position. Jump up: Pull arms down, breathe, land: kick motion, hold: glide. 10 reps.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Full body coordination. Combines all breaststroke elements. Feel the rhythm: pull-breathe-kick-glide.

exercises.name: Two-Kick Sequence Drill
exercises.description: Stand, simulate: First kick (small) with arm entry, Second kick (large) with arm push. Say 'Enter-kick, Push-kick.' 20 reps.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Practice two-kick timing muscle memory. Feel the smaller first kick, larger second kick. NEVER single kick. The rhythm becomes automatic.

exercises.name: Full Stroke Simulation
exercises.description: Stand bent forward. Complete butterfly sequence: Enter-kick, Pull-kick-push, Recover. Continuous motion. 10 reps continuous.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Whole stroke timing. No pauses between phases. Feel the continuous rhythm. Arms and body connected as one unit.

exercises.name: Metronome Drill
exercises.description: Stand, perform arm stroke simulation to metronome beat (60-80 BPM). Match motion to rhythm.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Rhythm development. Consistent tempo. Continuous motion matching the beat. Develops the timing sense for butterfly.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Slam
exercises.description: Stand, lift med ball overhead, slam down forcefully. 3 sets of 10.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Power for pull phase. Accelerate through motion (same as butterfly pull). Full body engagement. Feel the power transfer from core to arms.

exercises.name: Push-ups
exercises.description: Standard push-ups. 3 sets of 10-15.

strokeFocusPoints.freestyle: General upper body strength for pulling.
strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Develops insweep power. Narrow hand position mimics breaststroke pull. Elbows stay high during push-down.
strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: General upper body strength. Butterfly is strength-dependent. Strong chest and arms power the pull.

exercises.name: Push-ups Narrow
exercises.description: Standard push-ups with hands close together under shoulders. 3 sets of 10.

strokeFocusPoints.breaststroke: Develops insweep power. Narrow hand position mimics breaststroke pull. Elbows stay high during push-down.

exercises.name: Pull-ups
exercises.description: Standard pull-ups or assisted. 3 sets of 5-10.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Back strength for pull phase. Lats are primary butterfly muscles. Strong back = powerful pull.

exercises.name: Burpees
exercises.description: Full burpee with jump. 3 sets of 10.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Full body power and endurance. Butterfly requires sustained power. Builds the conditioning for continuous effort.

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Plank position, drive knees forward rapidly. 3 sets of 30 seconds.

strokeFocusPoints.butterfly: Core endurance for dolphin motion. Rapid hip motion similar to dolphin kick. Core stability throughout.
